summary.headline: Best opportunity today: a productized MTD-for-Income-Tax onboarding and automation service for UK sole traders, landlords and small accountancy firms.
summary.best_opportunity: Making Tax Digital onboarding, records cleanup and lightweight filing automation for sole traders, landlords and accountants
summary.why_now: HMRC guidance says sole traders and landlords over £50,000 qualifying income must use Making Tax Digital for Income Tax from 6 April 2026, with the £30,000 threshold following from 6 April 2027. That is a near-term compliance trigger, it affects fragmented buyers, and it can be sold as a setup fee plus recurring support without needing heavy product build upfront.

top_opportunities.name: Making Tax Digital onboarding and automation for sole traders, landlords and accountants
top_opportunities.description: Sell a fixed-scope service that cleans up records, connects bookkeeping software, builds simple receipt and expense workflows, and adds recurring quarterly MTD support for sole traders, landlords and small accountancy firms.
top_opportunities.why_now: Live GOV.UK guidance is unusually concrete: HMRC says qualifying income above £50,000 moves onto MTD for Income Tax from 6 April 2026 and above £30,000 follows from 6 April 2027, and compatible software is required. GOV.UK's 2024 business population release also says the UK had 5.5 million businesses at the start of 2024 and 5.45 million were small, so the addressable small-business base is large.
top_opportunities.buyer: Sole traders, small landlords, bookkeepers, small accountancy practices
top_opportunities.recurring_revenue: High - setup fee plus monthly or quarterly compliance support
top_opportunities.founder_fit: Excellent for a solo engineer because the first version is mostly workflow setup, integrations, dashboards and client onboarding rather than a full accounting platform.
risks: Accounting buyers may prefer recognised software brands unless the offer is tightly scoped around migration and admin reduction
You must stay careful not to position yourself as giving regulated tax advice when the offer is really software setup and process automation
top_opportunities.verdict: Best overall right now because the compliance deadline creates urgency, the buyers are numerous, and the offer can start service-first with fast cash flow.

top_opportunities.name: AI lead-response and admin automation for trades businesses
top_opportunities.description: Productize missed-call text-back, instant lead reply, quote follow-up, scheduling handoff and invoice chasing for plumbers, electricians, HVAC and other trades firms.
top_opportunities.why_now: Live vendor pages show an active market, which is useful evidence of real spend. Flowio's UK trades page currently promises faster lead response and admin reduction, while Tradify says it is trusted by 55,000+ tradespeople globally. That combination suggests demand is real, but many smaller firms still buy outcomes rather than software projects.
top_opportunities.buyer: Plumbers, electricians, roofers, heating engineers, small facilities and maintenance firms
top_opportunities.founder_fit: Very strong for a solo engineer because the stack is mostly integrations, messaging, CRM glue and reporting.
risks: There is visible competition, so the offer needs a narrow niche and clear ROI language
Some trades firms have messy lead tracking, which can make value harder to prove unless you instrument the funnel
top_opportunities.verdict: Still one of the fastest routes to revenue if sold around recovered jobs and reduced office admin instead of generic AI consulting.

top_opportunities.name: AI lead qualification and viewing-booking automation for estate and letting agents
top_opportunities.description: Build a productized layer that replies to portal leads, qualifies intent, books viewings, routes hot leads into the CRM and produces simple branch-level reporting.
top_opportunities.why_now: Current UK property-software pages show the category is moving now, not later. Alto's live guide compares AI lead management for UK estate and letting agents, and Street's current comparison page frames AI around compliance, out-of-hours handling and reporting. That is a good signal that buyers already understand the problem, even if competition is no longer low.
top_opportunities.buyer: Independent estate agencies, letting agencies, property managers
top_opportunities.recurring_revenue: High - monthly branch or seat-based retainer
top_opportunities.founder_fit: Strong if you can stay focused on lead handling and reporting rather than trying to replace the core estate CRM.
risks: CRM incumbents are already adding AI features, so a thin add-on can get squeezed
Property buyers can care about compliance and data quality, which raises implementation friction
top_opportunities.verdict: Good niche B2B opportunity, but best pursued as a tight lead-response layer rather than a broader proptech build.

top_opportunities.name: Conversion-focused local-business websites bundled with automation
top_opportunities.description: Sell quote funnels, lead capture, instant follow-up, simple CRM sync and owner reporting for local businesses instead of brochure websites.
top_opportunities.why_now: GOV.UK's business population release says 5.45 million of the UK's 5.5 million businesses were small at the start of 2024. That huge base keeps local-business web and workflow work alive, but the practical opportunity is only attractive when the website is tied to a measurable funnel and a recurring support plan.
top_opportunities.buyer: Independent trades, local professional firms, clinics, small agencies, home-service operators
top_opportunities.recurring_revenue: Medium to high - hosting, reporting and automation support
top_opportunities.founder_fit: Strong if you prefer shipping quickly and can sell on enquiries booked rather than hours worked.
risks: Generic web design is crowded and can collapse into one-off low-margin work
Without lead tracking and follow-up automation, recurring revenue is weaker
top_opportunities.verdict: Viable now, but only if positioned as a revenue system with recurring optimisation rather than a website project.

top_opportunities.name: Landlord and letting compliance workflow automation
top_opportunities.description: Offer a lightweight system for document collection, tenant communications, reminders, issue logging and reporting for private landlords and smaller letting operators.
top_opportunities.why_now: Live GOV.UK housing pages show the private rented sector remains large at 4.7 million households, and the 2024 English Private Landlord Survey says 45% of landlords owned one rental property while 38% owned two to four. That fragmentation matters because smaller landlords are more likely to need operational help, though the direct evidence for software buying urgency is weaker than for MTD.
top_opportunities.buyer: Small landlords, small letting agencies, portfolio landlords, property managers
top_opportunities.recurring_revenue: Medium to high - per-portfolio support or software fee
top_opportunities.founder_fit: Good for a technical founder willing to stay narrow and build a simple operations layer instead of a full property management platform.
risks: Buyer urgency is real but less explicit than the MTD deadline, so sales may take more education
Property-management incumbents already cover parts of this workflow
top_opportunities.verdict: Worth testing, especially via landlord and letting niches, but evidence for immediate cash is weaker than the top three ideas.

best_pick.name: Making Tax Digital onboarding and automation for sole traders, landlords and accountants
best_pick.why_it_wins: It has the clearest live trigger, the least need for speculative product building, and the best fit with part-time solo execution. The offer can start as a paid migration and workflow service, then turn into recurring support and eventually a micro-SaaS layer once repeat patterns are obvious.
validation_plan: Pick one wedge first: either landlords with basic self-assessment needs or small accountancy firms that want a migration helper for MTD clients.
Build a simple demo showing receipt capture, expense categorisation, quarterly reminder workflow and a lightweight client status dashboard.
Publish a fixed-price offer with a clear scope such as MTD readiness audit, software setup and first-quarter support.
Do direct outbound to 30 to 50 local accountants, bookkeepers and landlord groups before building custom product features beyond reporting and onboarding.
best_pick.first_offer: MTD readiness setup for sole traders and landlords: records cleanup, software connection, reminder workflows and first filing support. £299-£799 setup plus £39-£149 per month depending on complexity.
best_pick.mvp: A thin internal tool plus off-the-shelf bookkeeping software, document capture, email or SMS reminders and a small status dashboard for client onboarding and quarterly check-ins.
customer_types: Sole traders over the MTD threshold
best_pick.do_today: Create a one-page MTD readiness offer, list 25 local accountants and 25 landlord-facing businesses, and start outreach with a fixed-price migration package today.
